title: Top Ten Adventure Games - Part Two
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t1UIIjuMirY

In this second part of Daniel (@dungeondive)'s top adventure games countdown, he reveals his final five picks spanning sci-fi sandbox adventures to dark, twisted campaigns. Daniel kicks off with Ziya: Legends of a Drift System—a cowboy bebop-style spacefaring sandbox where you can become a merchant, trader, explorer, or pirate. Climbing the list, Roombound appears as a classic that exemplifies the genre perfectly, with its incredible expansions and survival mechanics. Fortune and Glory follows as the ultimate globe-trotting adventure, packed with press-your-luck mechanics that create genuine drama.

But Daniel's top two games are where things get truly special. Kingdom Death Monster earns the number two spot with its unmatched uniqueness—a dark, challenging, mature game that treats male and female characters equally while delivering visceral combat and consequences. Finally, Daniel's all-time favorite adventure game is Dungeon Degenerates: The Hand of Doom, a first board game so exceptional that Daniel considers it superior to veteran designers' offerings. Beyond the stunning art, Daniel praises the bold game design and innovative die system, alongside a campaign system that respects player choice.

----

What adventure game mechanics—whether press-your-luck, exploration tables, or character progression—have changed how you approach dungeon crawling and narrative gaming?
